reasoning_content:
Now I can explain why SUB-261326-01-R0 is connected to so many clauses. The spec section 26 13 26 has many individual clauses (at least 40+ unique clause IDs just from this spec). Let me count them from the text I've read:

From page 1: 1.1.1.A, 1.2.1, 1.2.2.A, 1.3.1.A, 1.4.1.A, 1.4.2, 1.4.3.A, 1.4.4.A, 1.4.5
From page 2: 2.1.1.A, 2.2.1.A, 2.2.1.B, 2.2.2.A, 2.2.2.B
From page 3: 2.2.3, 2.2.4, 2.3.1.A, 2.3.1.B, 2.3.1.C, 2.3.1.D, 2.3.1.E, 2.3.1.F, 2.3.2.A, 2.3.3.A, 2.3.3.B
From page 4: 2.3.4.A, 2.3.5.A, 2.3.6.A, 2.3.7.A, 2.3.7.B, 2.3.7.C, 3.1.1.A, 3.2.1.A

That's about 35+ clauses in the spec. And the graph node shows 76 total connections (8+53+9+6). 

The spec section 26 13 26 covers the complete MV switchgear system. This submittal package (Trident Switchgear) is the equipment submittal that needs to demonstrate compliance with EVERY clause in that spec section. Each of those spec clauses becomes a requirement that the submittal must address.

The high number (53 MISSING_EVIDENCE and 6 NEEDS_REVIEW) also suggests the submittal didn't adequately address all spec requirements, which is why it's flagged as needing review.

Let me check the margins page for more detail on this.
